reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P network by sending valid DataColumnSidecars. Go's randomized map iteration at lines 79-82 causes cellsIndices to be unsorted when passed to RecoverCellsAndKZGProofs, violating the c-kzg-4844 library requirement for sorted indices. This breaks PeerDAS reconstruction functionality, affecting all Prysm nodes (31% of network). All 3 FP gates passed: not dead code (active callers in sync layer), P2P untrusted path (not trust boundary issue), and in-scope for Fusaka PeerDAS audit.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: EIP-7594 PeerDAS - cell indices must be sorted for reconstruction
